decisions.title: Adopt Claude Code AI assistant across all Azimuth services
decisions.context: ENG-8925 and ENG-10132 show we're adding Claude Code to GitHub actions and web scripts. This could accelerate development velocity but needs standards for AGENTS.md documentation (ENG-9340) across all backend services.

arguments.for: Potential 20-30% velocity improvement on routine tasks; standardization via AGENTS.md ensures consistency; helps with complex migrations like DLv3 parquet schema work
arguments.against: Requires developer training; security review needed for FedRamp/GovCloud environments; may create dependencies on external AI services

decisions.title: Prioritize CSRS (Channelized Storage Read Service) over legacy DLv2 paths
decisions.context: Heavy CSRS investment visible: ENG-10157 (deduplication), ENG-9989 (integration tests), ENG-9493 (DataFusion parquet reads), ENG-9986 (SchemalessV2). Shadow comparison issues (ENG-10117) indicate production readiness questions. This is core to 'Nail the Core' - reliable data foundation.
decisions.blockedBy: ENG-10117 dedup asymmetry must be resolved; need migration plan for data-batcher flows (ENG-9954)

arguments.for: Modern architecture supports multi-channel parquet (ENG-9423); enables catalog update service (ENG-9427); required for scale (ENG-10150 targets 5M pts/sec)
arguments.against: Production parity issues still emerging; dual-running systems increases operational complexity; customer-facing bugs possible during transition

decisions.title: Fast-track FedRamp/GovCloud deployment for federal customer expansion
decisions.context: Multiple FedRamp signals: ENG-9846 (FIPS SHA-256), ENG-10156 (AWS workspace provisioning), ENG-9939 (Windmill in GovCloud), ENG-10155 (DNSSEC rotation docs). This aligns with 'Embed Deeper' strategy for high-value government customers.

arguments.for: Unlocks federal customer segment; demonstrates enterprise maturity; builds moat via compliance certifications; GovCloud Windmill enables data science workflows in secure environments
arguments.against: Significant engineering overhead for parallel infrastructure; compliance audits expensive; may slow feature velocity on core product

decisions.title: Resolve Explore V2 data source UX confusion blocking adoption
decisions.context: ENG-10158 (spotlight data source selector), ENG-4589 (dimension selection), ENG-10093 (broken Grafana sharelinks), ENG-10161 (partial bucket exclusion) all point to Explore V2 usability gaps. North Star metric 'Decisions Captured' requires users successfully query data.

arguments.for: Direct impact on North Star metric; reduces support tickets; improves trial-to-paid conversion; enables self-serve analytics
arguments.against: May require redesign iteration cycles; backend filter API changes (ENG-10138) add scope; risks breaking existing user workflows

decisions.title: Standardize global navigation patterns before Canvas launch
decisions.context: ENG-10084 (global nav breadcrumb bar) suggests IA updates. With Canvas as a key 2026 product, need consistent navigation between Explore, Canvas, and future Sift Edge interfaces. Impacts 'Embed Deeper' by reducing cognitive load.

arguments.for: Prevents technical debt accumulation; easier onboarding for multi-product users; cleaner API for embedding Sift in customer tools
arguments.against: Requires coordination across teams; may delay individual product releases; risk of over-engineering before user feedback

decisions.title: Invest in operational automation for data infrastructure reliability
decisions.context: ENG-9904 (compaction job automation), ENG-10154 (impulse downsampling cron), ENG-9898 (ambient transaction error handling) show operational maturity needs. Sift Edge (ENG-9436 exits on SQL lock) highlights production stability gaps.

arguments.for: Reduces on-call burden; prevents data quality incidents; enables scale without headcount; builds customer trust
arguments.against: Diverts resources from new features; may require platform rewrites; benefits accrue slowly over time

timeAudit.weekSummary: High meeting load (32hrs) limiting deep work on CSRS parity issues and Explore UX refinements. Consider batching sync discussions.
alerts: Only 8 focus hours this week - consider blocking Thursday/Friday afternoons for uninterrupted work
3 one-on-ones today may signal team needing directional clarity on FedRamp vs core product priorities
ENG-10117 (CSRS shadow comparison) requires deep technical analysis - schedule 4hr block next week
